audit_status.source_base: Greek Philippians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for unity/humility, Messiah hymn, working out salvation, shining as lights, Timothy/Epaphroditus.

verses.restored: Therefore if there is any encouragement in Messiah, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship/participation of Spirit, if any inward affections and compassions,
complete my joy: that you think the same, having the same love, joined in soul/life, thinking the one thing,
nothing according to selfish ambition or empty glory, but in humility counting one another as surpassing yourselves,
not looking each to one's own things, but each also to the things of others.
Let this mind be in you which also was in Messiah Jesus,
who, existing in form of the Divine, did not regard equality with the Divine as something to be seized/exploited,
but emptied himself, taking form of servant, coming to be in likeness of humans; and being found in shape as human,
he humbled himself, becoming obedient until death, even death of a cross.
Therefore also the Divine highly exalted him and graced him with the name above every name,
so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bend, of heavenly and earthly and under-earthly ones,
and every tongue should confess that Jesus Messiah is Lord, into glory of Divine Father.
So then, my beloved, just as you always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation/healing with fear/reverence and trembling,
for the Divine is the One working in you both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
Do all things without grumblings and arguments,
so that you may become blameless and innocent, children of the Divine without blemish in the midst of a crooked and distorted generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world,
holding fast the word of life, into my boast for the day of Messiah, that I did not run in vain nor labor in vain.
But even if I am being poured out as drink offering upon the sacrifice and service of your trust, I rejoice and rejoice together with all of you.
And in the same way you also rejoice and rejoice together with me.
But I hope in Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I also may be of good soul/life, knowing the things concerning you.
For I have no one equal-souled who will genuinely care for the things concerning you.
For all seek their own things, not the things of Jesus Messiah.
But you know his tested character, that as child with father he served with me in the good news.
Therefore I hope to send this one immediately, whenever I see the things concerning me.
And I have been persuaded in the Lord that I myself also will come soon.
But I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my sibling and coworker and fellow soldier, your apostle/messenger and servant of my need,
since he was longing for all of you and distressed because you heard that he was sick.
For indeed he was sick, near death; but the Divine showed mercy to him, and not to him only but also to me, lest I should have grief upon grief.
Therefore I sent him more eagerly, so that seeing him again you may rejoice and I may be less grieved.
Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such ones in honor,
because through the work of Messiah he came near to death, risking his soul/life, so that he might fill up what was lacking in your service toward me.

translation_notes: The Messiah hymn centers non-exploitative Divine form: equality with Divine is not seized but expressed through self-emptying service.
Work out salvation/healing is held together with Divine working within; this is participation, not self-salvation.
